blocks.body: Integers include all whole numbers and their negatives. Adding them requires attention to sign.
The distance of a number from zero on the number line. Always non-negative. Written |n|. Examples: |5| = 5, |-5| = 5, |0| = 0.
Same Sign Rule: When adding two numbers with the same sign, add the absolute values and keep the sign. Examples: 4 + 6 = 10, (-4) + (-6) = -10.

Different Sign Rule: When adding numbers with different signs, subtract the smaller absolute value from the larger, and keep the sign of the number with the larger absolute value. Example: -8 + 3 = -5 (because |-8| > |3|, keep the negative).

blocks.label: Example 1
blocks.body: -5 + (-3): same signs (both negative) -> add absolute values: 5 + 3 = 8 -> answer is -8.

blocks.label: Example 2
blocks.body: 7 + (-10): different signs -> |10| > |7| -> subtract: 10 - 7 = 3 -> keep negative sign -> -3.

blocks.label: Example 3
blocks.body: -4 + 9: different signs -> |9| > |4| -> subtract: 9 - 4 = 5 -> keep positive sign -> 5.
Think of a number line: positive means move right, negative means move left. Adding a negative is the same as moving left.
